steps.title: Identify how the study contradicts the narrative
steps.content: The popular narrative emphasizes migration to California, but the study “complicates this popular narrative”—a direct contradiction. The transition must show how the study's finding differs from what the narrative emphasizes.

steps.title: Match the transition to the contradiction
steps.content:
“More often” directly conveys the contradiction: it states that migrants actually stayed in the region more frequently than the narrative suggests—the precise way the study complicates the popular account.

The other choices either create illogical meaning (“for this reason” suggests circularity), add information without showing contrast (“additionally”), or redundantly repeat the earlier contrast word (“nevertheless”).

This matches the correct answer — B is correct.
